title: Podocarpus minus
intro: Podocarpus minus, also known as the Alpine Podocarp, is a coniferous evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Podocarpus minus include podocarpus resin, which is rich in lignans and sesquiterpenes, including podocarpine, podocarpic acid, and other terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made from Podocarpus minus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Podocarpus minus is a species of coniferous plant in the family Podocarpaceae. It is a member of the genus Podocarpus, which is a group of coniferous plants native to the Southern Hemisphere. The family Podocarpaceae, to which Podocarpus minus belongs, is a group of coniferous plants commonly known as yellowwoods. The order in which Podocarpus minus is classified is Pinales, which is an order of coniferous plants. Podocarpus minus is also classified as a type of gymnosperm, a group of seed-producing plants that are characterized by the absence of flowers and fruits.
